claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you'd like to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for helping me with this. I'm looking to claim for my son Alex. He's 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Since Alex is 18 years old, I need to ask a few more questions to determine your eligibility.

For children aged 16 or over, Child Benefit can usually continue if they stay in approved education or training.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training, such as:
- A-levels or equivalent
- Scottish Highers
- NVQs up to Level 3
- A course at a college or university that started before they turned 19?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and isn't doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since Alex is 18 and not in approved education or training, I have a couple more questions.

Has Alex recently (within the last 20 weeks) left education or training and registered with either:
- A government-sponsored careers service, or
- The armed services?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he hasn't registered with any careers service or the armed forces. He just finished school and that's it.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. I have one more question to check all possibilities.

Is Alex currently:
- Working 24 hours or more a week, or
- Receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work that many hours - he's not working full-time or anything like that. And he's not getting any benefits in his own name either.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I also need to confirm a couple of things about your own circumstances.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. And just to confirm, does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Unfortunately, based on the rules, I need to share some news that may not be what you were hoping for.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm afraid you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why:

**For Alex (age 18):**
- While you meet the basic requirements (you live in the UK and Alex lives with you), Child Benefit for children aged 16 or over can only continue if they remain in **approved education or training** until they turn 20.
- Since Alex has finished school and is not currently in any approved education or training, he no longer qualifies.
- The option to continue Child Benefit for 20 weeks after leaving education (by registering with a careers service or armed forces) only applies to 16 and 17 year olds, so this doesn't apply to Alex at 18.

**What you could do:**
If Alex decides to return to approved education or training in the future (before he turns 20), you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit again at that point. Approved education includes courses like A-levels, NVQs up to Level 3, or certain college courses.

I hope this helps clarify things. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old, which means he falls into the category of children aged 16 or over. For this age group, Child Benefit can normally only continue if the child stays in approved education or training until they turn 20. Alex is not currently in any approved education or training. Additionally, Alex has not registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services within the relevant timeframe (which would allow Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks for 16-17 year olds - but this doesn't apply as Alex is 18). Since Alex is over 16, not in approved education or training, and doesn't meet any of the exceptions for continued eligibility, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them, meeting the basic residency and responsibility requirements. However, since Alex is 18 years old and is not in approved education or training, he no longer qualifies for Child Benefit. Child Benefit for children aged 16 or over requires them to remain in approved education or training, which Alex is not.
